2603.27768.title: TailNLG: A Multilingual Benchmark Addressing Verbalization of Long-Tail Entities
2603.27768.paper_summary: The automatic verbalization of structured knowledge is a key task for making knowledge graphs accessible to non-expert users and supporting retrieval-augmented generation systems. Although recent advances in Data-to-Text generation have improved multilingual coverage, little attention has been paid to potential biases in the verbalization of rare entities, frequently known as long-tail entities. In this work, we present the first systematic study of long-tail entities in Data-to-Text generation. We introduce TailNLG, a new multilingual benchmark in English, Italian, and Spanish, built from Wikidata and covering entities with varying levels of popularity. We evaluate three different families of large language models in zero-shot settings and compare their performance on rare versus common entities, as well as against the established WebNLG benchmark. Our results reveal a consistent bias against long-tail entities: embedding-based scores are lower, and model uncertainty is higher for rare entities. We further show that the impact of long-tail entities varies across models and languages, and that existing evaluation metrics do not consistently capture these differences, highlighting the need for more reliable evaluation frameworks.
2603.27768.paper_summary_zh: 自動化的結構化知識口語化是使知識圖譜對非專業用戶可訪問並支持檢索增強生成系統的關鍵任務。儘管最近在數據到文本生成方面的進展改善了多語言覆蓋，但對於稀有實體的口語化潛在偏見卻鮮有關注，這些實體通常被稱為長尾實體。在這項工作中，我們提出了數據到文本生成中長尾實體的首次系統性研究。我們介紹了TailNLG，一個新的多語言基準，涵蓋英語、意大利語和西班牙語，基於Wikidata構建，並涵蓋不同受歡迎程度的實體。我們在零樣本設置中評估三種不同類型的大型語言模型，並比較它們在稀有實體與常見實體上的表現，以及與已建立的WebNLG基準的比較。我們的結果揭示了對長尾實體的一致偏見：基於嵌入的分數較低，且稀有實體的模型不確定性較高。我們進一步顯示，長尾實體的影響在不同模型和語言之間有所變化，現有的評估指標並未始終如一地捕捉這些差異，突顯了對更可靠評估框架的需求。
2603.27768.author: Lia Draetta et.al.
2603.27768.authors: Lia Draetta, Michael Oliverio, Virginia Ramón-Ferrer, Pier Felice Balestrucci, Flaviana Corallo, Carlos Badenes-Olmedo, Alessandro Mazzei, Marco Antonio Stranisci, Rossana Damiano
